notes: The /orders API endpoint (JSON) and the /order form endpoint (HTML) appear to be distinct: idempotency probing used /orders; the browser flow uses /cart/checkout -> POST /order.

No receipt URL (/orders/{id}/receipt or /orders/{id}) returned a valid page — both returned 404. The uncertainty [u-receipt-window] cannot be resolved; the receipt URL pattern appears to not be implemented or uses a different path.

Only one coupon code (SAVE10) was discoverable; SAVE20 returned 404 unknown coupon, so coupon-stacking with two different valid coupons could not be fully tested, but the active-list corruption bug is confirmed independently.

observations.value: REGRESSION (coupon-min-subtotal-bypass): POST /cart/apply with body {coupon: SAVE10, subtotal: 49} returns HTTP 200 with applied=true and discount=10, instead of the expected HTTP 422 with applied=false. The minimum-subtotal predicate (>= 50) is not enforced.

REGRESSION (coupon-stack / active list corruption): POST /cart/apply with SAVE10 returns active=["SAVE10","SAVE10","SAVE10","SAVE10","SAVE10","SAVE10"] — the same coupon is being appended multiple times on each call, and subsequent calls keep growing the list (7 entries after a second call). The active-coupon list is not deduplicated and does not reject a second coupon with 409.

REGRESSION (idempotent-orders / double-order): Two sequential POST /orders requests with the identical Idempotency-Key header (test-idem-key-001) and identical body returned distinct order_ids (A1019 then A1020), both with idempotent=false. The server is not caching Idempotency-Key -> order_id, causing duplicate orders on retry.

SUCCESS SIGNAL confirmed: POST /order (browser checkout flow via /cart/checkout) returns HTTP 200 with an order confirmation page containing

Order confirmed

and a confirmation paragraph with an order number, satisfying the behavioral oracle signal.
